note: Archive/Wiedijk100Theorems/Konigsberg.lean:75 — theorem not_isEulerian proves the Königsberg bridge graph (7 bridges, 4 landmasses) has no Eulerian trail; this IS the Seven Bridges of Königsberg result.
verify_note: Confirmed against mathlib4 checkout: file docstring reads 'The Königsberg bridges problem', graph is the actual 4-landmass/7-bridge mediant construction, and not_isEulerian at line 75 (not 74) proves no Eulerian trail exists — exact is honest.

note: Mathlib/Data/Complex/Basic.lean:34 — structure Complex : Type where re : ℝ; im : ℝ. The complex plane IS the set of complex numbers ℂ = Complex.
verify_note: Overclaimed: Wikidata Q328998 'complex plane' is described as the 'geometric representation of the complex numbers', distinct from Q11567 'complex number' (which the brain already links to Q328998 via a depends edge). Mathlib's `Complex` formalizes the algebraic field of complex numbers, i.e. Q11567's territory, not the geometric-representation concept the QID actually denotes — exact is too strong here.

note: Mathlib/Geometry/Polygon/Basic.lean:27 — structure Polygon (P) (n) has vertices : Fin n → P with no convexity requirement; a convex polygon is a special (unformalized) case of this general n-gon structure.
verify_note: Confirmed: Polygon P n at line 27 has only a vertices field, no convexity predicate anywhere in the file (the Convex.Between import is used for nondegenerate-edge machinery, not convexity); Wikidata confirms Q3259505 is specifically the convex case, so 'generalization' honestly describes Polygon as the broader unconstrained structure.

note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/Fermat.lean:33 defines fermatNumber n = 2^(2^n)+1 and :127 (pepin_primality) gives a primality test for it, but no named predicate for 'Fermat number that is prime' exists — nearby building blocks, not the concept itself.
verify_note: Confirmed: fermatNumber defined at line 34 (note says 33, off-by-one but immaterial) and pepin_primality at line 131 (note says 127); no Fermat-prime predicate exists in the file. 'related'/low-confidence is an honest, non-overclaiming description.

note: Mathlib/Probability/Moments/Basic.lean:62 — centralMoment X p μ is the general p-th central moment; skewness is the normalized 3rd central moment (centralMoment X 3 μ / variance^(3/2)), which itself is not formalized.
verify_note: Confirmed: centralMoment X p μ defined at line 62 exactly as described, no skewness/normalized-3rd-moment predicate anywhere in the file; 'generalization' at low confidence is an honest, appropriately hedged claim.
